title: Module 1 Syllabus — Introduction to GIS
description: Course syllabus with module schedule grading rubric and required datasets for Introduction to GIS for Environmental Science.

title: QGIS 3.34 Getting Started Guide
description: Step-by-step introduction to QGIS 3.34 LTR interface including layer panel toolbars CRS settings and the processing toolbox. Covers loading vector and raster data.

title: Map Design Reference — Reef Health Color Schemes
description: Reference sheet of recommended diverging and sequential color palettes for reef health and biodiversity maps. Includes ColorBrewer guidance and accessibility considerations for colorblind viewers.

title: R Spatial Analysis Environment Setup
description: Step-by-step guide for installing and configuring the R spatial stack: sf terra tmap ggplot2 spdep and leaflet. Includes troubleshooting tips for macOS and Windows.

title: Geocomputation with R — Online Book
description: Lovelace Nowosad and Muenchow Geocomputation with R — open-access reference textbook for all R spatial analysis tasks in Module 2.

title: Sentinel-2 Band Reference and NDWI Formula Sheet
description: Quick reference for Sentinel-2 band wavelengths spatial resolution and spectral index formulas including NDWI NDVI FAI (floating algae index) and turbidity proxy. Used in Units 2–3.

title: 2026 GIS Certification Program Requirements and Pathway Guide
description: Official certification requirements document listing all five modules capstone deliverable standards grading policies and the December 2026 completion deadline. Read before submitting the pathway agreement (cw_404).

title: Kaneohe Bay Sample Dataset v1 (GeoPackage)
description: Orientation sample GeoPackage containing three layers: reef_polygons (habitat classification), monitoring_points (survey station locations), and bathymetry_contours (5m intervals). CRS: EPSG:4326 (WGS 84).

title: macOS GDAL Fix Script (fix-GDAL.sh)
description: Shell script to fix the QGIS GDAL provider path on macOS Sonoma. Run from Terminal after QGIS installation if the provider is missing. See ann_012 for context.

title: CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance (Carroll et al. 2020)
description: Excerpt from Carroll SR et al. 2020 The CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance. Data Science Journal 19(1). Required reading for cw_415. Discusses Collective Authority Responsibility and Ethics in data use.

title: 2025 GIS Certification Program Orientation Archive
description: Archived orientation materials for the 2025 cohort. Includes 2025 cohort schedule QGIS 3.28 setup guide and original sample dataset documentation. Retained for reference.

title: Module 2 Syllabus — Spatial Analysis with QGIS and R
description: Full course syllabus for Spatial Analysis with QGIS and R including unit schedule weekly deliverables grading rubric and office hours schedule. R 4.4+ and QGIS 3.34 LTR required.

title: Marine Spatial Planning Case Study — Kāne'ohe Bay
description: Dataset package for the Module 2 marine spatial planning case study. Includes a GeoPackage of Kāne'ohe Bay reef polygons DLNR benthic classification layer and a CSV of CFI transect point coordinates. Students produce a habitat suitability map for coral restoration prioritization.

title: Module 3 Syllabus — Remote Sensing Fundamentals
description: Course syllabus for Remote Sensing Fundamentals covering passive and active platforms image analysis workflows and the drone survey project deliverable. Google Earth Engine and SNAP software required.

title: Landsat Change Detection Lab — Kāne'ohe Bay 2013–2025
description: Guided lab exercise using Landsat 8 and Landsat 9 imagery to detect coral habitat change in Kāne'ohe Bay between 2013 and 2025. Students apply band compositing NDWI and supervised classification in Google Earth Engine. Pre-processed image stack provided via Google Drive.

title: Capstone Rubric and Deliverable Guide 2026
description: Grading rubric and step-by-step deliverable guide for the GIS Certification Capstone. Students must produce (1) a methods narrative (1500 words) (2) a publication-quality map layout (3) a reproducible QGIS project file and (4) a 10-minute recorded presentation. Rubric weights: methods 30% map quality 30% reproducibility 20% presentation 20%.

title: Georeferencing Reef Photo Transects — Tutorial Video
description: Recorded walkthrough (38 min) covering georeferencing photo-transect images in QGIS using GPS track logs as ground control. Topics: importing GPX files setting CRS assigning GCPs calculating RMS error and exporting a rectified image for habitat classification. Directly applicable to capstone reef datasets.

title: Coordinate Reference Systems Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference card for the most commonly used CRS in Pacific marine science: WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) UTM Zone 4N (EPSG:32604) Hawaii State Plane Zone 3 (EPSG:102661) and NAD83. Includes a table of appropriate CRS by use case (field data collection vs. area calculation vs. web mapping).

title: Moran's I and Spatial Autocorrelation — Lecture Slides
description: Slide deck (42 slides) for the Unit 6 spatial autocorrelation lecture. Covers global and local Moran's I LISA cluster maps and the spdep package in R. Includes worked example using bleaching score data from a hypothetical reef monitoring dataset.

title: Habitat Classification Field Guide — Kāne'ohe Bay Species
description: Illustrated field guide to the 12 most common coral and benthic habitat types in Kāne'ohe Bay for capstone habitat classification projects. Covers Porites lobata Porites compressa Montipora capitata Acropora spp. crustose coralline algae turf algae Gracilaria salicornia and invasive Kappaphycus. Includes spectral signatures for remote sensing classification.
